data.title: Tokenized Real-World Assets Show Resilience Amid Cryptocurrency Market Turmoil
data.description:
Demand for tokenized real-world assets (RWAs) has shown significant growth over the past month, despite the broader cryptocurrency markets experiencing substantial selling pressure. According to Cointelegraph, this trend highlights the sector's resilience and its expanding institutional presence.

Data from RWA.xyz indicates that the total value of onchain RWAs increased by 13.5% over the past 30 days. This growth is attributed to higher asset issuance, with more tokenized securities being introduced to public blockchains, and an increase in the number of unique wallet addresses holding these assets, suggesting a rise in participation. As of February 16, major blockchain networks tracked by RWA.xyz, including Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Solana, recorded significant increases in tokenized asset value, with Ethereum leading at approximately $1.7 billion in net growth, followed by Arbitrum at $880 million and Solana at $530 million.

Excluding stablecoins, the net growth in tokenized securities such as Treasurys, private credit, and other yield-bearing instruments has accelerated over the past 30 days. Tokenized US Treasurys and government debt remain the largest RWA category, with over $10 billion in outstanding onchain products. Flows into these instruments continued during the period, while tokenized stocks and exchange-traded products also posted gains.

The steady demand for tokenized RWAs indicates deeper institutional participation, as asset managers increasingly utilize public blockchains to issue and settle tokenized versions of traditional financial products. Tokenized money market funds are evolving beyond simple yield vehicles and are beginning to serve as collateral in certain trading and lending markets. Major institutions, including BlackRock, JPMorgan, and Goldman Sachs, have become active participants in this space. BlackRock recently made its first formal move into decentralized finance by introducing its USD Institutional Digital Liquidity Fund (BUIDL) tokenized US Treasury fund to Uniswap.

This growth contrasts sharply with the broader cryptocurrency market, which has lost approximately $1 trillion in market value over the past month, underscoring the relative stability of yield-bearing tokenized assets. The total crypto market has continued to unravel since October, with losses intensifying in January. Derivatives markets have been a key source of stress, with a large-scale deleveraging event in October triggering broader weakness across digital assets. Conditions have yet to fully recover, and sentiment remains fragile even as equities continue trading near record highs.

data.title: Tokenized RWAs climb 13.5% despite $1T crypto market drawdown
data.description:
Tokenized real-world assets added 13. 5% in 30 days, led by increasing activity on Ethereum, Arbitrum and Solana, even as the broader crypto market lost $1 trillion in value.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/tokenized-rwas-climb-despite-crypto-market-rout?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: SBI Holdings CEO Denies $10B XRP Holdings
data.description:
SBI Holdings’ Chairman and CEO Yoshitaka Kitao publicly rejected circulating claims that the Japanese financial group holds approximately $10 billion in XRP tokens, clarifying that the company does not own those tokens directly and instead holds an equity stake in Ripple Labs. com.
source: https://techgaged.com/sbi-holdings-ceo-denies-10b-xrp-holdings/

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Suggests Prediction Markets as Stablecoin Alternative
data.description: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has proposed that prediction markets could provide price stability akin to stablecoins, potentially reducing reliance on fiat currency. According to NS3.AI, Buterin envisions a system where users can hedge future expenses through personalized prediction market shares, offering a sustainable alternative to traditional money. Industry leaders support this view, seeing prediction markets as evolving from entertainment to essential economic information infrastructure.

data.title: Germany Considers Age Restrictions on Social Media Amid Tensions with Trump
data.description: Germany is considering implementing age restrictions on social media platforms, a decision that could heighten tensions with U.S. President Donald Trump. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential for increased friction between the two nations. The proposed regulations aim to protect younger users from harmful content and ensure safer online environments. This move aligns with broader European efforts to regulate digital platforms and enhance user safety. The introduction of such measures may lead to diplomatic discussions, as the U.S. administration has previously expressed concerns over European digital policies impacting American tech companies. The outcome of Germany's decision could influence similar regulatory actions across Europe.

data.title: New Zealand Central Bank Governor Faces Inflation Challenges
data.description: New Zealand's newly appointed central bank Governor, Anna Breman, is set to make her debut this week, with expectations that interest rates will remain unchanged. Bloomberg posted on X, Breman's focus will be on addressing the country's economic outlook amid persistent inflation pressures. Her approach to managing inflation will be closely watched by economists and market analysts, as she navigates the complexities of the current economic environment. Breman's decisions could have significant implications for New Zealand's financial stability and growth prospects.

data.title: Vitol Proposes Gas-to-Power Project in South Africa
data.description: Vitol, a leading energy and commodities company, is part of a consortium that has proposed a gas-to-power project in South Africa. Bloomberg posted on X that this initiative comes as South Africa seeks to transition from its aging coal-fired power stations to more sustainable energy sources. The project aims to address the country's energy challenges by providing a reliable and cleaner alternative to coal. South Africa's move towards gas-to-power solutions is part of a broader strategy to diversify its energy mix and reduce carbon emissions. The consortium's proposal is expected to play a significant role in the country's energy transition efforts.

data.title: Bakkt Jumps 7% After Nexo Picks It for U.S. Comeback
data.description:
Shares of Bakkt (BKKT) surged 7% on February 13, 2026, closing at $10. 77, after crypto lender Nexo confirmed it will use Bakkt’s infrastructure for its return to the U. S. market
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bakkt-jumps-7-after-nexo-picks-it-for-u-s-comeback/

data.title: Ethereum ICO Wallet Awakens After 10 Years – What Happened?
data.description:
An Ethereum (ETH) ICO-era wallet just woke up after roughly 10. 6 years of inactivity and attempted to send 1 ETH to Gemini, according to on-chain trackers. The transaction failed due to low gas, but estimates suggest the wallet still holds about 1,430 ETH originally acquired for about $443
source: https://techgaged.com/ethereum-ico-wallet-awakens-after-10-years-what-happened/

data.title: Hong Kong Grants First Crypto License Since June to Victory Fintech
data.description: Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has granted a license to Victory Fintech, marking the first approval for a crypto company since June. According to NS3.AI, this development indicates a gradual regulatory reopening in Hong Kong's cryptocurrency sector. The issuance of this license could potentially impact investor sentiment and stimulate further crypto business activities within the region.

data.title: PI Rockets, Then Pulls Back: What’s Driving the Swings?
data.description:
Pi Network’s IOU token is cooling off after a sharp weekend rally, with price now trading near $0. 17 following a volatile 48-hour stretch. After briefly surging 35% on February 15, PI faced heavy resistance near the $0. 20 level and has since pulled back roughly 11–15% over the past 24 hours
source: https://www.ethnews.com/pi-rockets-then-pulls-back-whats-driving-the-swings/

data.title: Payjoin Foundation Achieves Nonprofit Status to Boost Bitcoin Privacy
data.description: The Payjoin Foundation, focused on advancing Bitcoin privacy through open-source software, has been granted 501(c)(3) nonprofit status. According to NS3.AI, this designation allows U.S. taxpayers to make tax-deductible donations, providing sustainable funding for the development of privacy protocols such as Async Payjoin. This advancement does not necessitate changes to the Bitcoin protocol. Several prominent Bitcoin wallets have already integrated or support the Payjoin Dev Kit, enhancing user privacy while maintaining compatibility.

data.title: Nexo Is Back in the U.S. After Three Years Away
data.description:
After nearly three years on the sidelines, digital asset lender Nexo has officially relaunched its services in the United States. The company’s return follows a period of regulatory disputes that forced its exit in late 2022. This time, Nexo is re-entering the market through a strategic partnership with Bakkt, aiming to ensure its offerings align […] The post Nexo Is Back in the U. S
source: https://www.ethnews.com/nexo-is-back-in-the-u-s-after-three-years-away/
